source.author: Cabannes, Yves
source.title: Participatory budgeting, a significant contribution to participatory democracy
source.note: Framing source for what participatory budgeting is and where it spread.

source.author: Wampler, Brian
source.title: Participatory budgeting, core principles and key impacts
source.note: Core principles cited in the introduction and the method rationale.

source.author: Fung, Archon
source.title: Putting the public back into governance
source.note: Anchors the trust argument about residents inside the process.

source.author: Gilman, Hollie Russon
source.title: Democracy reinvented, participatory budgeting in America
source.note: Reading for American cases that match the midsize population band.

source.author: City of Syracuse
source.title: Adopted budget and capital plan
source.note: Local grounding and a real capital plan to read closely.

source.author: Pew Charitable Trusts
source.title: How cities engage residents in spending decisions
source.note: Useful survey framing for resident engagement measures.

source.author: Touchton, Michael
source.title: Inequality and participatory budgeting outcomes
source.note: To review for the self selection limitation in the method section.

source.author: Bryan, Frank
source.title: Real democracy, the New England town meeting
source.note: Background on direct participation outside the budgeting frame.

source.author: GovLab NYU
source.title: Crowdlaw and local participation toolkits
source.note: Practical toolkit reading for how cities run the cycle.

source.author: Baiocchi, Gianpaolo
source.title: Participatory institutions and the politics of spending
source.note: Cited on how institutions shape where voted money goes.
